I'm busy right now. I understand. Life happens all the time. What time can we follow up? Tomorrow morning or tomorrow afternoon? Which one's better? I can do tomorrow evening. Great. What time? 4, 6? I can do 4. Okay, is that Eastern Pacific? Great. And the email I have on file, that's gonna work to send a counter notification over. Okay, cool. Anything besides a zombie or nuclear apocalypse or catastrophe, you're gonna be showing up, right? Okay, awesome. I'm gonna deliver a immense value to you on the call. I'll talk to you soon. Bye. If somebody says I'm too busy right now, you lock down the appointment. Lock down a follow-up. Hey, I'm too busy right now. Awesome. I agree. Everybody's busy. Let's do this. When's a good time where you're gonna be able to set aside 5 to 10 real minutes to do this? Well, I'm not gonna have 5 to 10 real minutes for you. I don't even know who you are or what you're doing. Sir, that's why we're actually setting aside this 5 to 10 real minutes. I'll send you some information, some introduction information between now and then that way you can get a clear idea about who I am, what I'm trying to do, and why I'm talking to you, and I promise you I'll deliver you immense value on the call that'll directly impact your business. Can we talk for that introduction tomorrow between 5 and 6 or are you more of a warning person? Okay, I guess I'll talk to you. I mean, be sure to shoot me over that email. I'll do tomorrow at 5. Okay, and what's your email, sir? My email says, great. And I'll be sure to shoot you over counter notification between now. Anything between a nuclear apocalypse and a zombie catastrophe of some kind, you're gonna be showing up to that meeting at 5, right? Perfect. I'll shoot you the information between now and then I'll shoot you the calendar invite. Should you have any questions between now and then or find anything else out about me that you need me to ask or air out on, be sure to give me a call. Talk to you tomorrow at 5. Lock down the appointment. Nobody's too busy for an appointment. If they just say they're too busy once again, it's a bluff objection. You got a real objection that they're not addressing. Be sure once again to immediately agree say, hey, I agree you're too busy. We're all too busy. That's why I'm actually calling you right now because I thought this would be a good time for you. I just need 5 to 10 real minutes. Do you have 5 to 10 real minutes later tonight? What do you want me to call you tomorrow morning? It's that simple. Lock it down. See you guys in the next video. This has been the objection handling series. It's out of phase number four, the sales and prospecting. Be sure to go back on each one of these whenever you need them, whenever you see fit and practice, practice, practice. Be sure to role play with people in real life or people in the DAP community to practice going back and forth with a sales pitch so you guys can get better internally. Use these objection handlers when people are giving you these objections in the market. If you guys have an objection that's not on this list, be sure to comment it below. We'll make it into a video and we'll add it right into the section. I look forward to seeing you guys in the next video.
